meta_title: For Freelancers: deployment workflow to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Deployment Workflow to Solve Stuck Apps
content.seo_description: Learn how to master your deployment workflow and overcome being stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop manually deploying. By automating your CI/CD pipeline using Antigravity for infrastructure-as-code and Claude Code for intelligent CLI orchestration, you convert brittle, basic apps into production-grade assets. This workflow ensures repeatable, high-speed deployments that impress clients and eliminate the 'it works on my machine' bottleneck forever.
content.intro:
You’ve spent days coding, but the momentum dies the moment you try to ship. You're stuck in 'tutorial hell' where your apps exist only on localhost, and the prospect of configuring servers, environment variables, and build pipelines feels like an insurmountable wall between you and your next paycheck. This is the invisible ceiling holding back every freelancer from scaling their impact.

But the era of manual deployment is over. By integrating Antigravity’s infrastructure automation with Claude Code’s command-line intelligence, you can treat deployment as a standardized, one-command process. This guide strips away the complexity, giving you a professional-grade deployment architecture that moves as fast as your code.

steps.title: Environment Variable Sanitization
steps.description: Standardize your secrets management to ensure your app never breaks during environment transitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure .env.example template and a validation script that checks for missing production keys before build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a validation script for environment variables'

steps.title: Automated Infrastructure Provisioning
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define your hosting environment as code, ensuring your production server is identical to your staging setup.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a cloud-agnostic deployment configuration for a containerized Node.js application including auto-scaling rules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize antigravity config for production deployment'

steps.title: Build Pipeline Orchestration
steps.description: Automate the build process so that every commit triggers a linting and testing suite before attempting deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a CI pipeline configuration that runs unit tests and build checks before triggering the deployment webhook.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions for automated build and test'

steps.title: Zero-Downtime Deployment Logic
steps.description: Implement a blue-green deployment strategy to ensure your users never see a 500 error while you update the app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a rolling update strategy for container orchestration to ensure zero-downtime during new version releases.

steps.title: Health Check & Rollback Automation
steps.description: Add a post-deployment verification step that automatically reverts the deployment if the health check fails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a health check endpoint monitor that triggers an automatic rollback if the deployment status is unhealthy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add automated rollback script to deployment pipeline'

pro_tips: Always separate your staging and production databases at the infrastructure level, never just by a flag.
Use ephemeral environments for every Pull Request to allow clients to preview features before merging to production.
Inject your logs into a centralized aggregator like Axiom or Datadog early; debugging without logs is a freelancer's death sentence.
Set your build timeout threshold to 300 seconds to catch inefficient build processes before they drain your compute budget.
Keep your infrastructure-as-code files in a separate repository from your application code to prevent accidental credential leakage.
content.next_steps: Once your pipeline is live, set up automated performance monitoring to ensure your app stays fast as your user base grows.
content.conversion_block: Stop building basic apps and start shipping production-grade SaaS. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
